data.epi_content:
"What?"

The words Vivian spoke to me after our long time apart were sharp.

In a moment of thinking I might die, I clumsily gathered magic power and embraced Ivan.

Ivan whimpered in fear.

Losing the skeletons is regrettable, but such things naturally accumulate when wandering through villages and encountering vagabonds and deserters.

Just before I could chant a spell to escape to my hideout, Vivian addressed me.

"Don't run away. You said you wanted to talk.

You kept telling people to relay messages to me. That you wanted to talk."

"So, have you finally developed some interest in talking?

When I was helpless and a pathetic cripple who couldn't do anything, you wouldn't listen to a word I said."

Vivian stared at me for a long while before sighing.

"Should I call you miss? Anyway, let me hear you out.

Why are you and Evan going to such lengths, struggling to kill people?

Johannes, I didn't know he was such a monster, but you even burned the Emperor to death."

So the Emperor burned to death.

Good.

I didn't particularly want to watch that disgusting man die anyway.

"This is how I survive. If the Emperor is dead, it seems things are mostly resolved.

Now you won't let Evan and me live, will you?"

"..."

Vivian didn't answer, just gritted her teeth.

After a long silence, Vivian spoke first.

"After committing such atrocities, do you still want to live?"

I answered without hesitation.

"Yes. Even though Evan and I haven't seen each other for years, we know we're both alive and can still see a future."

Hearing this, Vivian snorted and said,

"You've grown so much since I saw you at the Academy."

Her face is simply beauty itself, and her chest and hips appear to be the standard of feminine form.

I glanced at Vivian's chest, then looked down. If I teased her about her small breasts, she'd probably just laugh it off.

"I suppose so, Crippled Witch."

"So that's what they call me these days."

"You've killed so many, after all.

Miss, isn't it strange to think you wouldn't earn a nickname?

A silver-haired, one-eyed, crippled black mage riding a one-eyed dog, with a beautiful face to boot—perfect for people to talk about."

"That's fortunate. Thanks to that, I've received and read several letters from Evan.

Though we can't meet because you're still surrounding the walls and blocking spells."

"Let's end this pointless chatter."

"Pointless? You're the ones wasting time unnecessarily."

Vivian tilted her head slightly, as if she didn't understand.

"Just let Evan and me go. I don't have any grand ideologies or anything.

If you do that, we'll hide away somewhere and live quietly."

"Do you think that makes sense?"

"Do you really think you can capture the capital?
data.epi_content2:
You send living soldiers, but the walls are defended by skeletons.

And when your soldiers die, they only increase the number of skeletons."

I created a small fireball and threw it at Vivian's feet.

It raised dust and the flames died down.

"Even when you occasionally throw your precious magic, it's useless.

If Evan were truly in danger, he would have found a way to escape."

I took out some tobacco from my pocket and put it in my mouth.

I recently found a good cigar and, having no proper flavoring, tried sweet potato flavor, which turned out surprisingly good, so I keep smoking it.

I gestured to Vivian, holding the tobacco between two fingers as if offering her one.

Vivian stared at it for a while, then knocked it away with her hand.

I picked up the fallen tobacco, dusted it off, and put it back in my pocket.

"Why, am I wrong?

You came looking for me and wanted to talk because you have no answers either.

All the opportunities to end this through dialogue have already been lost."

Vivian frowned at those words.

"What opportunities were lost..."

I cut her off and answered.

"I was almost raped, and Evan took care of that evil male student, which should have been enough.

Even if it seemed suspicious and hard to accept.

We were planning to kill just the Crown Prince and then flee somewhere to live peacefully."

"Who would believe that?

Erica, Miss Erica. Look at yourself.

What once looked cute now appears to everyone as nothing but an evil black mage.

Your white hair is dirty with dried blood, your face is covered in dust, you reek of blood, and that left foot I destroyed... damn, I should have completely eliminated you then."

I wiggled my right foot in front of Vivian, mocking her.

"Why, this time I'll give you my right foot and run away."

Nothing happens because we've been through too much together for either of us to fall for such offers.

We're simply not attacking each other.

We only slightly raise our voices and the intensity of our words. Vivian knows that if she casts a spell to capture me, I'll immediately flee, so she just hurls curses at me.

"Evan and you must be punished.

Your limbs should be torn off, you should be burned, and like many others, you should be cursed to bleed slowly to death.

How could just two people, just two, cause such terrible things..."

I suppose whoever created this world made it so fragile that just two people with different intentions could cause such chaos.

"I was sick of it.

Terribly, horribly sick of it."

Suddenly, it becomes hard to breathe.

"Curse? Yes, I've been cursed. I was cursed even before you mentioned it!!!"

Having seen countless people shouting and raging, she doesn't show the fresh reactions she used to at the Academy.

"Yes, limbs? I've already had limbs torn off.

My arm was torn off, and that damn Johannes diligently burned it with fire.

Then, saying I might die from the pain, he poured drugs into my nostrils."

Vivian responds indifferently to those words.

"You said you wanted to talk, but were you just looking for me all this time to spout nonsense?"

I created water in the air, sprinkled it around, and showed her the memory I had seen.

Vivian would know better whether it was true or not.
data.epi_content3:
Of my two outstretched arms, one flies off and is immediately burned.

The other three are soon cut off and burned too, and as the Crown Prince's—no, the Emperor's—finger approaches my eye, the screen turns completely black.

Vivian still looks at the darkened screen in disbelief, then looks down at me sitting there.

She seemed flustered.

The direction of our conversation had completely changed.

From negotiation to accusation.

"As if you haven't experienced it, hah. As if you haven't.

Don't stand there looking down at me, sit down, you bitch."

At those words, Vivian slightly contorted her expression, then sat down in front of me.

"Fine, I'll hold back the smoke. Listen to my story even if it's boring.

I'll explain carefully why I would just hide away if you let me go.

I'll give you reasons. I'll make excuses for why I've turned the world into such a mess and why I deserve to be cursed."

The smoke is terribly stale.

So I created water in the air to drink, then stared at a sprouting potato for a while before putting it in my mouth and crunching on it.

I'm past the point where eating such things would make me sick.

"Yes, at first I died because of you."

"What does that mean..."

"Evan liked you.

I hated you so much that I hit you once, and Evan came to me and went crazy.

And very fortunately for you, that annoying girl's family collapsed."

I take out the gun from my pocket.

Even if I shoot, Vivian won't get hit anyway.

"At first, I tried to die by cutting my wrists in warm water, but it hurt.

So I took a gun and shot myself, but I didn't die with one shot.

I made a hole in my neck, made bubbling sounds, and just died.

Slowly, all alone, where no one could see me."

The bathtub water turning red begins to appear.

After some time, the screen goes dark.

"Even if you show me this and explain why you did such things, do you think I'll just let you and Evan go?"

"Of course not.

You kept me imprisoned by your side until I died.

So I just burned to death. In front of you. I wonder if you felt anything."

I approach Vivian and poke her with my finger.

That great magician sits still without resisting.

"I don't know how many times I've died because of you.

I've tried to think it was all because I wasn't good enough, but I just can't.

From my family's downfall, to why I couldn't live normally at the Academy, because of the people around you I couldn't even breathe, couldn't do anything, was bound, and when I tried to escape from the harassment, I was pushed back in, and even when I wanted to hide, I was dragged out... wait, am I talking too much? Yes, it's long. I've lived too long. Why, when I should have died over and over, does it seem so offensive that I'm standing before you with my life intact?"

As I spoke, my throat suddenly closed up.

"I don't know what I'm saying. Anyway, the cursed death you speak of.

The death that sinners receive, I've experienced it all. Even though I hadn't committed any great sins.

But now that I'm actually killing people, death seems to have moved further away."

Vivian's face is expressionless.

But her lips were trembling slightly.

"What do you think? Doesn't it seem better to just let us go?"

As if the situation was very different from what she had imagined in her mind.

Ah, how detestable.
